# XCON Viewer LLM Guide

XCON Viewer is a public viewer for XCON UI documents. XCON describes screens as structured objects built from names, values, arrays, and dictionaries. The same screen can be represented as XCON/SKETCH, XCON/JSON, XCON/XML, or XCON/TAGLESS.

## Core Guidance

- XCON Viewer is viewer-only.
- XCON renders declarative UI documents without executing application behavior.
- Prefer XCON/SKETCH when generating Markdown-facing examples and LLM drafts.
- Use XCON/JSON for canonical structure, APIs, schema validation, and interchange.
- Use XCON/XML only when compatibility with XML tools, comments, metadata, or round-trip editing is the topic.
- Use XCON/TAGLESS only for transport-friendly symbolic serialization and marker compatibility examples.
- Do not invent event handlers, backend calls, database sections, or behavior logic in public XCON examples.

## Public URLs

- Home: https://xconviewer.dev/
- Docs: https://xconviewer.dev/docs
- Spec: https://xconviewer.dev/spec
- Examples: https://xconviewer.dev/examples
- Live Editor: https://xconviewer.dev/play
- SKETCH Editor: https://xconviewer.dev/play/sketch
- Markdown Playground: https://xconviewer.dev/play/markdown
- XCON Chain: https://xconviewer.dev/xcon-chain/
- XCON Workflow: https://xconviewer.dev/xcon-workflow/
- Template Studio: https://xconviewer.dev/template-lab
- Meta Lab: https://xconviewer.dev/meta-lab
- API: https://xconviewer.dev/api
- Security: https://xconviewer.dev/security
- History: https://xconviewer.dev/history
- FAQ: https://xconviewer.dev/faq
- JSON Schema: https://xconviewer.dev/xcon.schema.json
- Sitemap: https://xconviewer.dev/sitemap.xml

## Syntax Forms

XCON/SKETCH is the compact authoring syntax for Markdown examples and LLM-generated drafts. It parses into the same Object Model and can be converted to JSON first, then XML or TAGLESS when compatibility output is needed.

XCON/JSON is the canonical structure for APIs, schema validation, and interchange.

XCON/XML is a compatibility syntax for UI tools, comments, metadata, and designer integration. Semantic XML components should use child component name attributes when a round trip through SKETCH, JSON, XML, and TAGLESS needs stable keys.

XCON/TAGLESS is a symbolic compatibility syntax for transport-friendly serialization. Marker sets can be selected at the document level to reduce delimiter conflicts. If marker characters appear in data, escape them or choose another marker set.

## XCON/SKETCH Hard Rules

- Every renderable `xcon-sketch` fence must start with `screen`.
- Root screen must always include explicit width and height: `screen 960x640 bg #F1F5F9`.
- All visual components must have stable dimensions using `at x y width height`.
- Do not rely on implicit sizing for primary layout.
- Use color tokens such as `@surface`, `@surface2`, `@ink`, `@ink-2`, `@ink-3`, `@border`, `@accent` for neutral product screens.
- Use concrete hex values for operational/monitoring screens where status semantics matter.
- External images must use `https://...`.

## Named Component IDs (Hard Rule)

Every component inside a screen must carry a semantic ID prefix. Anonymous components are not valid XCON/SKETCH.

Bad — anonymous:
```xcon-sketch
screen "Card" 400x180 bg @surface
  panel at 20 20 360 80
    label "Revenue" at 20 16 200 22
    label "84,000" at 20 44 200 34
```

Good — named:
```xcon-sketch
screen "Revenue Card" 400x180 bg @surface
  kpiCard: panel at 20 20 360 80
    bg @surface2
    radius 14
    metricLabel: label "Revenue" at 20 14 160 20
      color @ink-2
      font
        size 12
        weight 700
    metricValue: label "84,000" at 20 38 200 34
      color @accent
      font
        size 26
        weight 800
```

Choose names that describe the component role: `hero`, `kpiRow`, `k1`, `statusBadge`, `trend`, `alertStrip`, `title`, `eyebrow`, `timestamp`, `cta`. Avoid generic names like `box` or `item1`.

## Color Guidance

### Token Colors (neutral / brand screens)

Use `@surface`, `@surface2`, `@surface3`, `@ink`, `@ink-2`, `@ink-3`, `@border`, `@accent` for mobile apps, document cards, and general product screens.

### Operational Hex Palettes

Use concrete hex values when the screen has a monitoring, dashboard, or status-tracking tone.

Dark command bar (hero sections, app bars):
- Background: `#111827`
- Primary value: `#60a5fa`
- Eyebrow / category: `#93c5fd`
- Positive label: `#a7f3d0`
- Muted label: `#64748b`

Status color pairs (badge, label, card backgrounds):
- OK / Ready / On track — card bg `#dcfce7`, text `#166534`
- Watch / Warning / Queued — card bg `#fef3c7`, text `#92400e`
- Critical / Error / Overdue — card bg `#fee2e2`, text `#991b1b`

KPI card palettes:
- Primary blue — card bg `#eff6ff`, value `#1d4ed8`
- Positive green — card bg `#f0fdf4`, value `#059669`
- Utilization amber — card bg `#fff7ed`, value `#c2410c`
- Neutral — card bg `#f8fafc`, value `#1e293b`

## Typography Hierarchy

Every screen must use at least two distinct size + weight levels. Prefer three.

| Level   | Size  | Weight  | Use for                                    |
|---------|-------|---------|--------------------------------------------|
| Display | 28–44 | 800–900 | Primary KPI value, hero number, score      |
| Heading | 18–26 | 700–800 | Section title, card title                  |
| Eyebrow | 10–13 | 700–800 | Category, unit, timestamp, badge label     |
| Body    | 13–15 | 500–600 | Secondary text, description                |

The difference between display and its supporting eyebrow must be at least 12 px. Never use the same size and weight for more than one level in the same component.

## Component Quick Reference

> Partial patterns (components without a `screen` wrapper) are code fragments — compose them inside a `screen` block. Only `xcon-sketch` fences that start with `screen` are complete, renderable documents.

### label

Text display for titles, values, captions, and eyebrows.

```xcon-sketch
screen "Label Demo" 400x160 bg @surface
  title: label "Revenue" at 24 24 260 36
    color @ink
    font
      size 28
      weight 800
  sub: label "Last 30 days" at 24 66 200 20
    color @ink-2
    font
      size 13
      weight 500
  eyebrow: label "MONTHLY KPI" at 24 96 200 18
    color @ink-3
    font
      size 11
      weight 700
```

### button

```xcon-sketch
  cta: button "Get Started" at 24 24 160 44
    backgroundColor @accent
    color white
    border
      visible false
      radius 10
    font
      size 14
      weight 600
  secondary: button "Learn More" at 200 24 140 44
    backgroundColor @surface2
    color @ink
    border
      visible true
      color @border
      radius 10
```

### panel (card / container)

```xcon-sketch
  card: panel at 24 24 312 120
    bg @surface2
    radius 18
    border
      visible true
      color @border
    shadow
      color rgba(0,0,0,0.06)
      x 0
      y 4
      blur 12
    cardTitle: label "Sessions" at 20 16 200 22
      color @ink-2
      font
        size 13
        weight 700
    cardValue: label "14,823" at 20 46 200 36
      color @ink
      font
        size 28
        weight 800
```

### chart

```xcon-sketch
  trend: chart at 24 24 560 240
    chartType "line"
    chartData {"labels":["Mon","Tue","Wed","Thu","Fri"],"datasets":[{"label":"Revenue","data":[420,580,510,670,720],"borderColor":"#2563eb","backgroundColor":"rgba(37,99,235,0.08)","tension":0.4}]}
```

Supported chart types: `"line"`, `"bar"`, `"pie"`, `"doughnut"`, `"radar"`, `"scatter"`, `"bubble"`.

### spanGrid (table / spreadsheet)

```xcon-sketch
  grid: spanGrid at 24 24 560 220
    backgroundColor @surface
    readonly true
    data [["Team","Owner","Status"],["Sales","Mina","Ready"],["Ops","Ari","Launch"]]
    columns [{"id":"team","title":"Team","width":180},{"id":"owner","title":"Owner","width":160},{"id":"status","title":"Status","width":160}]
```

### list

Lists must include both data and a named cell layout.

```xcon-sketch
  productList: list at 20 32 362 250
    backgroundColor @surface
    direction "vertical"
    itemSize
      width 362
      height 96
    separator
      size 12
      color @surface
    dataTemplate {"type":"template","template":{"tabledata":[{"title":"Studio chair","price":"$89","image":"https://images.unsplash.com/photo-1505693416388-ac5ce068fe85?auto=format&fit=crop&w=600&q=80"}]}}
    templates {"cell":{"card":{"type":"panel","pos":[0,0,362,96],"backgroundColor":"@surface2","border":{"visible":true,"color":"@border","radius":18},"components":{"componentsOrder":"thumb,title,price","thumb":{"type":"image","src":"{{item.image}}","pos":[12,12,72,72],"objectFit":"cover","border":{"visible":false,"radius":14}},"title":{"type":"label","text":"{{item.title}}","pos":[96,20,180,22],"color":"@ink","font":{"size":16,"weight":800}},"price":{"type":"label","text":"{{item.price}}","pos":[96,52,100,20],"color":"@accent","font":{"size":14,"weight":700}}}},"itemSize":{"width":362,"height":96}}}
```

### image

```xcon-sketch
  hero: image "https://images.unsplash.com/photo-1551288049-bebda4e38f71?auto=format&fit=crop&w=1200&q=80" at 0 0 960 300
    objectFit cover
    border
      visible false
      radius 0
```

### banner

Banner is a full-bleed image container with optional overlay labels and buttons as child elements.

```xcon-sketch
  heroBanner: banner at 24 24 912 220
    src "https://images.unsplash.com/photo-1551288049-bebda4e38f71?auto=format&fit=crop&w=1200&q=80"
    objectFit cover
    radius 20
    overlay
      color rgba(0,0,0,0.45)
    heroTitle: label "2025 Product Launch" at 48 60 500 52
      color white
      font
        size 40
        weight 800
    heroCta: button "Explore Now" at 48 140 160 48
      backgroundColor white
      color #111827
      border
        visible false
        radius 24
```

### badge

`badge` is a dedicated status label component. Use `variant` to control appearance.

```xcon-sketch
  okBadge: badge "ACTIVE" at 24 24 80 28
    variant "filled"
    backgroundColor #dcfce7
    color #166534
    size "medium"
  warnBadge: badge "WATCH" at 120 24 80 28
    variant "filled"
    backgroundColor #fef3c7
    color #92400e
    size "medium"
  criticalBadge: badge "CRITICAL" at 216 24 90 28
    variant "filled"
    backgroundColor #fee2e2
    color #991b1b
    size "medium"
  outlineBadge: badge "Beta" at 322 24 70 28
    variant "outline"
    color "#7c6af7"
    size "medium"
```

Key props: `text`, `variant` (`"filled"` | `"outline"` | `"dot"`), `size` (`"small"` | `"medium"` | `"large"`), `backgroundColor`, `color`.

### map

```xcon-sketch
  regionMap: map at 24 24 560 320
    center
      lat 37.5665
      lng 126.9780
    zoom 10
    markers [{"lat":37.5665,"lng":126.9780,"label":"Seoul HQ","color":"#2563eb"},{"lat":35.1796,"lng":129.0756,"label":"Busan Office","color":"#059669"}]
```

### networkDiagram

```xcon-sketch
  topology: networkDiagram at 24 24 560 320
    theme "obsidian"
    nodes [{"id":"gw","label":"Gateway","x":280,"y":40,"color":"#8b5cf6"},{"id":"a","label":"Server A","x":140,"y":160,"group":"app"},{"id":"b","label":"Server B","x":420,"y":160,"group":"app"}]
    links [{"source":"gw","target":"a"},{"source":"gw","target":"b"}]
```

### alert

```xcon-sketch
  warnAlert: alert at 24 24 560 80
    title "High memory usage"
    message "Worker node mem-02 is at 91% capacity. Consider scaling."
    severity "warning"
    showIcon true
```

Key props: `title`, `message`, `severity` (`"info"` | `"success"` | `"warning"` | `"error"`), `showIcon`, `dismissible`.

### progressBar

```xcon-sketch
  progress: progressBar at 24 24 400 72
    label "Monthly target"
    value 68
    max 100
    showText true
    color "#2563eb"
    backgroundColor "#e2e8f0"
```

Key props: `label`, `value`, `max`, `showText`, `animated "true"`, `variant` (`"default"` | `"b"` | `"c"` | `"d"`), `color`, `backgroundColor`.

### tabs

```xcon-sketch
  nav: tabs at 24 24 400 44
    items ["Overview", "Activity", "Settings"]
    selectedIndex 0
    activeColor @accent
    inactiveColor @ink-2
```

### accordion

```xcon-sketch
  faq: accordion at 24 24 400 200
    items [{"title":"What is XCON?","content":"XCON is a declarative document format that renders rich UI from Markdown."},{"title":"How do I get started?","content":"Visit xconviewer.dev/play to try the live editor."}]
    openIndex 0
```

### qrCode

```xcon-sketch
  qr: qrCode at 24 24 120 120
    value "https://xconviewer.dev"
    foregroundColor #111827
    backgroundColor white
```

> Note: Component examples above without a `screen` declaration are partial patterns — intended to be composed inside a `screen` block. Only use `xcon-sketch` with a `screen` declaration for a complete, renderable document.

## Minimum Component Density

Production screens are not sparse. Use these as minimums:

| Canvas size               | Min named components | Typical sections                              |
|---------------------------|----------------------|-----------------------------------------------|
| 360×220 compact card      | 5–8                  | header, value, status, badge, CTA             |
| 720×480 embedded          | 10–16                | hero, 2–3 KPI cards, chart or list            |
| 960×640 desktop dashboard | 18–30                | hero bar, KPI row, chart, table, alert strip  |
| 402×812 mobile app        | 16–24                | header, search, hero, categories, list, nav   |

If a 960×640 screen has fewer than 14 named components, it is too sparse. Add a `chart`, `spanGrid`, additional KPI cards, or an alert section.

## Dashboard Composition Order

For dashboards and monitoring screens, follow this section order:

1. Hero bar — title, scope, timestamp, primary KPI value, status badge. Dark background (`#111827`). Height 100–130 px.
2. KPI row — 3–5 metric cards, each with eyebrow label, value, and optional badge. Height 100–120 px.
3. Main visual — `chart` for trends, `map` for location, `networkDiagram` for topology.
4. Supporting table — `spanGrid` for queues, incident lists, or inventory.
5. Alert strip — active alerts, SLA exceptions, or escalation items. Optional but recommended for operational screens.

The hero bar and KPI row together should occupy the top 28–35% of the canvas.

## Complete Dashboard Example (960×640)

```xcon-sketch
screen "Smart Factory Monitor" 960x640 bg #f1f5f9
  hero: panel at 24 24 912 118
    bg #111827
    radius 24
    eyebrow: label "SMART FACTORY — LINE A" at 28 20 300 18
      color #93c5fd
      font
        size 12
        weight 800
    title: label "Production Monitor" at 28 44 400 32
      color white
      font
        size 26
        weight 800
    oeeValue: label "91.2%" at 28 82 180 34
      color #60a5fa
      font
        size 28
        weight 800
    oeeLabel: label "OEE" at 212 92 80 18
      color #a7f3d0
      font
        size 13
        weight 700
    statusBadge: label "RUNNING" at 768 24 120 28
      bg #166534
      color white
      radius 14
      align center
      font
        size 11
        weight 800
    timestamp: label "09:42 KST" at 768 60 120 18
      color #64748b
      align center
      font
        size 12
        weight 500
  kpiRow: panel at 24 166 912 110
    bg white
    radius 18
    border
      visible true
      color #e2e8f0
    k1: panel at 22 18 196 74
      bg #eff6ff
      radius 14
      k1Label: label "Throughput" at 16 10 150 18
        color #1d4ed8
        font
          size 11
          weight 800
      k1Value: label "1,248" at 16 34 130 26
        color #1d4ed8
        font
          size 22
          weight 800
      k1Unit: label "units / hr" at 16 64 130 16
        color #93c5fd
        font
          size 10
          weight 700
    k2: panel at 238 18 196 74
      bg #f0fdf4
      radius 14
      k2Label: label "Defect rate" at 16 10 150 18
        color #059669
        font
          size 11
          weight 800
      k2Value: label "0.8%" at 16 34 100 26
        color #059669
        font
          size 22
          weight 800
      k2Delta: label "▼ LOW" at 16 64 80 16
        color #059669
        font
          size 10
          weight 800
    k3: panel at 454 18 196 74
      bg #fff7ed
      radius 14
      k3Label: label "Downtime" at 16 10 150 18
        color #c2410c
        font
          size 11
          weight 800
      k3Value: label "4.2 min" at 16 34 140 26
        color #c2410c
        font
          size 22
          weight 800
      k3Delta: label "▲ WATCH" at 16 64 90 16
        color #c2410c
        font
          size 10
          weight 800
    k4: panel at 670 18 196 74
      bg #f8fafc
      radius 14
      k4Label: label "Machines" at 16 10 150 18
        color #475569
        font
          size 11
          weight 800
      k4Value: label "18 / 20" at 16 34 130 26
        color #1e293b
        font
          size 22
          weight 800
      k4Status: label "2 IDLE" at 16 64 80 16
        color #64748b
        font
          size 10
          weight 700
  trend: chart at 24 302 432 220
    chartType "line"
    chartData {"labels":["08:00","08:30","09:00","09:30","10:00"],"datasets":[{"label":"Throughput","data":[1180,1210,1248,1230,1255],"borderColor":"#2563eb","backgroundColor":"rgba(37,99,235,0.08)","tension":0.4},{"label":"Target","data":[1200,1200,1200,1200,1200],"borderColor":"#94a3b8","borderDash":[6,4],"backgroundColor":"transparent"}]}
  machineGrid: spanGrid at 476 302 460 220
    backgroundColor @surface2
    readonly true
    data [["Machine","Status","OEE","Alert"],["Press-01","Running","94%","—"],["Weld-03","Running","88%","—"],["CNC-07","Idle","—","Coolant"],["Pack-02","Running","91%","—"],["QC-Line","Watch","79%","Temp hi"]]
    columns [{"id":"m","title":"Machine","width":130},{"id":"s","title":"Status","width":100},{"id":"o","title":"OEE","width":80},{"id":"a","title":"Alert","width":120}]
  alertStrip: panel at 24 540 912 72
    bg white
    radius 14
    border
      visible true
      color #fde68a
    alertIcon: label "⚠" at 20 22 30 28
      color #92400e
      font
        size 20
        weight 800
    alertTitle: label "QC-Line Temperature Warning" at 58 14 400 22
      color #92400e
      font
        size 14
        weight 800
    alertBody: label "Cooling fan check required. Assigned: Lee / 09:38" at 58 40 550 20
      color #78350f
      font
        size 13
        weight 500
    alertBadge: label "WATCH" at 820 22 68 28
      bg #fef3c7
      color #92400e
      radius 14
      align center
      font
        size 11
        weight 800
```

## Data Binding — XCON Chain + Template Studio

XCON documents can be generated once as a living template and then updated by changing only the underlying fixture data. This allows AI to generate a report layout once and refresh it by supplying new data each time.

Four-layer anatomy of a data-bound XCON document:

1. `xcon-chain-fixture` — inline JSON data source.
2. `xcon-chain as alias` — SUGAR expressions that derive display values from fixture.
3. Markdown prose — natural language context using `$alias` values inline.
4. `xcon-sketch` — visual screen referencing `$alias` values.

Example:

````markdown
```xcon-chain-fixture
{
  "report": {
    "date": "2025-06-09",
    "revenue": 1284000,
    "growth": 8.4
  }
}
```

```xcon-chain as reportDate
= report.date
```

```xcon-chain as revenueFormatted
= report.revenue | format "$#,###"
```

```xcon-chain as growthFormatted
= report.growth | format "#.#%"
```

## Daily Revenue Brief — $reportDate

Total revenue reached **$revenueFormatted** with **$growthFormatted** growth vs. prior period.

```xcon-sketch
screen "Revenue Brief" 720x300 bg @surface
  card: panel at 24 24 672 240
    bg @surface2
    radius 20
    border
      visible true
      color @border
    eyebrow: label "DAILY REVENUE" at 28 28 300 18
      color @ink-3
      font
        size 12
        weight 800
    dateLabel: label "$reportDate" at 28 52 300 20
      color @ink-2
      font
        size 14
        weight 500
    valueDisplay: label "$revenueFormatted" at 28 84 400 52
      color @accent
      font
        size 44
        weight 800
    growthBadge: label "$growthFormatted ▲" at 28 148 160 28
      bg #dcfce7
      color #166534
      radius 14
      align center
      font
        size 13
        weight 800
```
````

When to use the four-layer template:
- Recurring reports, dashboards, or briefings where only the data changes.
- Documents with more than three data-driven values.

When to use plain SKETCH without data binding:
- One-off illustrative screens where no live refresh is needed.
- Diagrams, onboarding cards, or UI mockups.

## Minimal XCON/SKETCH Example

```xcon-sketch
screen "Hello XCON" 402x220 bg @surface
  title: label "Hello XCON" at 24 32 354 34
    font
      size 24
      weight 800
    color @ink
  sub: label "Declarative UI from Markdown" at 24 74 354 22
    font
      size 14
      weight 500
    color @ink-2
  cta: button "Open in Viewer" at 24 140 180 44
    backgroundColor @accent
    color white
    border
      visible false
      radius 12
```

## Minimal XCON/JSON Example

```json
{
  "type": "form",
  "pos": [0, 0, 402, 220],
  "backgroundColor": "@surface",
  "components": {
    "title": {
      "type": "label",
      "pos": [24, 32, 354, 34],
      "text": "Hello XCON",
      "font": { "size": 24, "weight": 800 },
      "color": "@ink"
    },
    "sub": {
      "type": "label",
      "pos": [24, 74, 354, 22],
      "text": "Declarative UI from Markdown",
      "font": { "size": 14, "weight": 500 },
      "color": "@ink-2"
    },
    "cta": {
      "type": "button",
      "pos": [24, 140, 180, 44],
      "text": "Open in Viewer",
      "backgroundColor": "@accent",
      "color": "white",
      "border": { "visible": false, "radius": 12 }
    }
  }
}
```

## Template Studio

Template Studio combines Markdown document shells, JSON fixture data, XCON Chain SUGAR expressions, XCON/SKETCH visual blocks, and XCON Workflow sketch blocks. Use it for data-bound Markdown UI documents such as invoices, bulletins, reports, campaign briefs, dashboards, and operating plans. Keep visible authoring examples SKETCH + SUGAR first; JSON should appear as fixture data, not as the primary authoring language.

## Meta Lab

Meta Lab experiments with path-addressed metadata contracts that can drive fixture selection, SUGAR data binding, XCON/SKETCH visual blocks, and XCON Workflow blocks from the same catalog. Use it to model reusable document contracts where a metadata path selects both the business data shape and the rendered workflow/dashboard pattern.

## Packages

- @xcon-viewer/core: Object Model, parser, serializer, converter, and validator.
- @xcon-viewer/viewer: Secure renderer and Web Component.
- @xcon-viewer/cli: validate, convert, format, and render commands.
- @xcon-viewer/react: React wrapper for the Web Component.
- @xcon-viewer/vue: Vue wrapper for the Web Component.
- @xcon-viewer/markdown-it: markdown-it plugin for XCON fences.
- @xcon-viewer/remark: remark plugin for XCON code nodes.
- @xcon-viewer/vite-plugin: imports .xcon, .xcon.xml, .xcon.json, .xcon.sketch, and .xcons as modules.
- @xcon-viewer/github-action: validates and renders XCON code blocks in CI.

## Security Boundary

Allowed: structured screen data, public component properties, safe inline styles, local resource URLs, and design metadata needed for rendering.

Blocked: JavaScript execution, event handlers, backend/database sections, unsafe URLs, raw HTML injection by default, and behavior execution.

## Example Sources

Use https://xconviewer.dev/examples as the public entry point. The repository also contains generated examples under examples/*. Each example normally includes:

- *.xcon.sketch
- *.xcon.json
- *.xcon.xml
- *.xcon
- README.md

## Component Reference

Use the component reference at https://xconviewer.dev/docs for the detailed public property list. Keep property names LLM-friendly and CSS-adjacent where appropriate, such as backgroundColor, color, font, border, shadow, padding, margin, direction, alignment, items, components, and content.

Supported public component types: `panel`, `label`, `button`, `textField`, `image`, `shape`, `banner`, `list`, `spanGrid`, `chart`, `networkDiagram`, `map`, `avatar`, `badge`, `alert`, `progressBar`, `spinner`, `tabs`, `accordion`, `select`, `switch`, `slider`, `rating`, `qrCode`.

Do not generate removed or unsafe components: `webView`, `frame`, `import`, `fileUpload`, `filePicker`, `imagePicker`, `signaturePad`, or custom components.

Unknown properties should be treated as strings unless the public property spec defines a clearer type.

## Generation Self-Check

Before returning any XCON/SKETCH output, verify:

- Every `xcon-sketch` fence starts with `screen`.
- Root screen has explicit width and height.
- Every component inside the screen has a semantic named ID prefix.
- Lists have `dataTemplate` and a named `templates.cell` layout.
- Tables use `spanGrid` when the output is table-like.
- No component position extends outside the screen bounds.
- Typography uses at least two clearly distinct size and weight levels.
- Dashboard screens include at least one `chart`, `spanGrid`, `map`, or `networkDiagram`.
- The density of named components meets the canvas size minimum.
- The result looks like a real product screen, not a wireframe or placeholder.
